goal.objective: Produce an implementation-ready architecture for a portable host-assisted IDE Run Loop over one approved V11 specialist package.

assumptions.statement: The external host persists immutable JSON-serializable session values.
assumptions.rationale: Core can remain deterministic and effect-free while runs remain restartable from caller-owned files.

assumptions.statement: V12 derives host instructions and evidence but performs no host runtime effect.
assumptions.rationale: This is the smallest usable IDE-neutral layer over V11 without repeating the universal runtime coupling.

assumptions.statement: One V12 run binds one approved V11 specialist package.
assumptions.rationale: Multi-package runtime graphs are unnecessary for the first usable run loop.

unresolvedDecisions.question: Which closeout facts can core derive without claiming host-owned tests, merge, persistence, or memory effects?
unresolvedDecisions.proceedRationale: The architecture pass can compare bounded options before any public API is frozen.

unresolvedDecisions.question: What is the smallest public operation set for session creation, inspection, handoff acceptance, routing, and closeout?
unresolvedDecisions.proceedRationale: Architecture specialists own a recommendation; implementation remains gated on integrated acceptance.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The design preserves exact package and handoff identity, least authority, caller persistence, and IDE/provider neutrality without claiming runtime effects.

evidenceRequirements.description: Preserve the exact authority, security, and portability review.

acceptanceCriteria.description: One implementation-ready decision maps operations, state, routes, tests, deferred behavior, and work units to V12 AC1-AC9.

evidenceRequirements.description: Preserve the integrated architecture decision brief.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The design has deterministic monotonic state, explicit outcomes, safe dependency readiness, and a bounded relationship to deferred runtime corrections.

evidenceRequirements.description: Preserve the exact lifecycle and recovery review.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The design gives an IDE one clear path from an approved package to visible launch, fan-in, integration, and closeout instructions.

evidenceRequirements.description: Preserve the exact product and IDE usability review.

contextSources.description: Deferred universal runtime-control-plane design evidence.
Accepted compiler-first boundary and deferred runtime list.
Normative V11 package, handoff, fan-in, and host contract.
Mandatory correction record for later runtime-adjacent work.
Existing bounded host-injected execution boundary.
Current workflow, interaction, parallelization, and integration rules.
Current raw handoff and dependency fan-in implementation.
Current visible IDE specialist workflow.
Current package, scripts, and installed-consumer surface.
Current public kernel export surface.
Released V11.1 public product and host boundary.
Independent findings against the coupled universal runtime.
Immutable V12 goal-synthesis snapshot.
Immutable V12 feature-spec snapshot.

forbiddenEffects: Do not write files, run tests, access the network, launch descendants, change Git state, or claim that core performs host effects.

workUnits.objective: Audit the proposed V12 layer for exact identity, least authority, immutable caller persistence, security, public API truth, and IDE/provider portability.

module.action: Define trust inputs and outputs, reject confused-deputy and stale-evidence paths, enumerate forbidden host claims, and return concrete schema/API/test obligations mapped to AC1-AC9.

workUnits.objective: Design a deterministic monotonic session lifecycle over the fixed V11 package DAG and separate it rigorously from the deferred universal scheduler.

module.action: Map states, transitions, non-pass routes, dependency readiness, serialization, replay, and failure boundaries against every relevant Round-4 correction; identify mandatory versus deferred behavior.
